reviews.review_title: This is the game that made me omni-man
reviews.review_description:
Let’s get this straight: Untangled is supposed to be a Spider-Man-type VR game… but the feeling it gives me? Yeah, this is low-key an Invincible power fantasy in disguise.

I start by eating a spider (already wild 💀), turning into “Web-Guy,” and getting dropped into a world where I can swing around using physics-based webs. You can climb basically anything, launch yourself across buildings, fight enemies, and mess around with other players. It’s multiplayer, it’s chaotic, and it doesn’t try too hard to control you—which is exactly why it works.

The movement is where everything clicks for me. It’s not slow or guided—it’s fast, loose, and built on momentum. I build speed quickly, fling myself across the map, and somehow recover mid-air like physics is just a suggestion. At first it feels messy, but once you get the hang of it, it turns into this smooth, almost instinctive flow where you’re chaining swings without even thinking.

And that’s when the feeling changes for me. I don’t feel like Spider-Man anymore—I feel like I’m operating on a completely different level than everything around me. The world starts to feel smaller, slower, like it can’t keep up. It’s the same kind of energy you get watching someone like Omni-Man in Invincible—not because I’m literally flying or smashing through buildings, but because of that sense of control and dominance over movement.

The multiplayer chaos honestly makes that even better for me. People are flying everywhere, bumping into each other, grabbing each other mid-air, and just doing random stuff instead of actual objectives. It’s less “hero simulator” and more “mess around and see what happens,” which somehow makes the power fantasy feel even stronger. I’m not playing a clean, cinematic hero—I’m just a menace with web powers and too much freedom.

It’s not perfect, though. The controls can feel janky at first, and the swinging definitely takes practice. It’s not super polished either, so you’ll notice some rough edges. But once the movement finally clicks, those issues matter way less because the core feeling is just that fun.

At the end of the day, Untangled isn’t the most advanced VR game out there, but it nails something way more important: the feeling of speed, freedom, and power. You go in expecting a Spider-Man experience, and you come out feeling like you were moving faster and freer than anything else in the world.

5/5 — I came to swing… I stayed because I felt unstoppable.

Anyways I hope the developer hears this which he probably will and I hope this could be a future update.

This game is also a really silly game if you’re looking for something like a battle glide VR at this game is probably the closest you’ll get to it. That’s free. This game does look like one of those games where you can tell it’s in beta or it looks a little new, but anything other than that this is a pretty good game I used to not like it very much but now I’m starting to get into it. Public lobbies are much more fun than just swinging around or flying around than private lobbies. Some suits are a little bit low quality and yeah, not all of them. There’s some really good looking ones but yeah, I think that’s it. ￼
